title: Billable weight and local rate-card price lookup (TRACT-DELIV-01)
description: §16.5.3: 'dim_divisor — billable = max(actual, L*W*H / divisor)'. TRACT-DELIV-01 asserts a leg's price is computed LOCALLY from a published RateCard, without a live call to the carrier. All three cases reuse zone 1's brackets from TRACT-WIRE-VEC-14's RateCard example. Units: §16 does not pin L/W/H or dim_divisor to a length unit; every case here is constructed so L*W*H/divisor divides exactly, so the (open, unspecified) rounding question never has to be answered to check the arithmetic (see `volumetric_weight`'s docstring). `dim_divisor` is read as the carrier's own calibration constant that already lands the result in the same unit as `actual` and `WeightBracket.max_grams` (grams) — a real carrier's published divisor is exactly the free parameter that makes that true for its own cm/kg convention; nothing here asserts a specific real-world divisor value is standard.

cases.name: actual weight dominates
cases.note: volumetric = 10*10*10 / 1000 = 1000/1000 = 1g (exact). billable = max(4000, 1) = 4000g. Zone 1's brackets (max_grams, price EUR minor units) are (1000,500) (5000,900) (20000,1800); the smallest max_grams >= 4000 is 5000 -> price 900.

cases.name: volumetric weight dominates
cases.note: volumetric = 100*80*125 / 1000 = 1000000/1000 = 1000g (exact). billable = max(200, 1000) = 1000g. Smallest bracket max_grams >= 1000 is exactly 1000 -> price 500 (the boundary is inclusive under the >= rule stated in the `select_bracket` docstring).

cases.name: tie — actual equals volumetric
cases.note: volumetric = 100*50*40 / 100 = 200000/100 = 2000g (exact), equal to actual (2000g). max(2000, 2000) = 2000g either way. Smallest bracket >= 2000 is 5000 -> price 900.
